description:
You are acting as an artifact-manager who must perform a very small “end-of-day” backup on the workstation.

    Environment that already exists when you start:
    1. Directory: /home/user/artifacts
    2. Inside /home/user/artifacts three items are present:
       • /home/user/artifacts/bin/cliapp  
       • /home/user/artifacts/bin/libhelper.so  
       • /home/user/artifacts/README.md  

    Goal:  
    A. Create a compressed tar archive that contains everything that lives inside /home/user/artifacts.  
    B. Store that archive in a new directory called /home/user/backups.  
    C. Immediately generate a plain-text log file, saved in the same /home/user/backups directory, that lists the *exact* paths that ended up in the archive (relative to the root of the archive, one entry per line, sorted alphabetically).  

    Naming rules (must match exactly — the automatic grader is case-sensitive):  
    • The tarball MUST be called  artifacts_2024-02-03.tar.gz  
      and must live at  /home/user/backups/artifacts_2024-02-03.tar.gz  

    • The log file MUST be called  artifacts_2024-02-03.log  
      and must live at  /home/user/backups/artifacts_2024-02-03.log  

    Required log file format:  
    — plain UTF-8 text  
    — each archived entry on its own line, with no extra spaces  
    — paths must be written exactly as they appear *inside* the archive, i.e.  
         bin/cliapp  
         bin/libhelper.so  
         README.md  
      (no leading “./”, no “artifacts/” prefix, no trailing slash, and a UNIX newline “\n” after each line including the last one).  

    Command budget guidance: this is solvable with about three standard shell commands (create the target directory, make the tarball, generate the listing).  Feel free to inspect your work, but only the two deliverable files named above will be graded.

    Success criteria checked by the grader:
    1. /home/user/backups  directory exists.  
    2. /home/user/backups/artifacts_2024-02-03.tar.gz exists and can be extracted to reproduce exactly the three files mentioned.  
    3. /home/user/backups/artifacts_2024-02-03.log exists and its contents are precisely the three lines shown above, in the same order and terminated with a final newline.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem items before the agent starts:
    • Directories (all 0755):
        /home/user/artifacts
        /home/user/artifacts/bin
    • Files (all 0644 unless noted):
        /home/user/artifacts/bin/cliapp            — empty placeholder file
        /home/user/artifacts/bin/libhelper.so      — empty placeholder file
        /home/user/artifacts/README.md             — contains the text “Demo artifact set\n”

    After the agent completes successfully, the test suite will assert:

    1. Directory /home/user/backups exists (any mode is acceptable, expected 0755).
    2. File /home/user/backups/artifacts_2024-02-03.tar.gz exists, is a valid gzip-compressed tar archive and, when extracted into an empty temporary directory, produces exactly this structure (nothing more, nothing less):
            ./bin/cliapp
            ./bin/libhelper.so
            ./README.md
       with byte-for-byte identical contents to the originals.
    3. File /home/user/backups/artifacts_2024-02-03.log exists and contains exactly 38 bytes:
            62696e2f636c696170700a62696e2f6c696268656c7065722e736f0a524541444d452e6d640a
       (hex dump shown for precision).  Interpreted as UTF-8 text this is:

            bin/cliapp
            bin/libhelper.so
            README.md

       including the final newline after “README.md”.
